sections.paragraph:
προγνώστης, ου, ὁ (cp. πρόγνωσις; PGM 5, 410) one who knows someth. beforehand, of God (Just. A I, 44, 11; D. 16, 3; 23, 2 al. Theoph. Ant. 2, 15 [p. 138, 2].—Of Apollo: Tat. 19, 3) πρ. τῶν πάντων one who knows everything beforehand 2 Cl 9:9.—DELG s.v. γιγνώσκω.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

someth. someth. = something

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 867
